titleStmt.title: SII 9.125: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 125. (A.R. No. 293 of 1918.) ON THE SAME SLAB.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 9[8]3, Plava, Jyēshṭha, amāvāsyā, Ādityavāra, Sūrya-grahaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1061 June 20, Wednesday, on which day there was a solar eclipse; Sunday is probably an error. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Trailōkyamalladēva and records that the Daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya, who was administering Mēgaṇaṅgeṟe in Kadambaḷike thousand, made a gift of land for the service of the god Svayambhu-Kalidēva. The poet Barmmadēva wrote this.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samastabhuvanāśraya śrī thvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirāja paramēśvaraṁ
paramabhaṭṭāraka kyābharaṇa śrīmattraiḷō
kyamalladēvara vijayarājyamuttarōttarā pravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkkatāraṁ
baraṁsaluttamire tatpādapadōpa jīvi samadhigatapañca mahāsabda mahāsāmaṁ
tādhipati mahāpracaṇḍadaṇḍanāyakaṁ vidvajjanābhīṣṭaphaḷapradāyakaṁ śrīmanmahāhērisaṁ
dhivigrahi paḷaperggaḍe daṇḍanāyakaṁ kēsimayyaṁgaḷ kadaṁbaḷike sāyirado
ḷagaṇa catugrāme mēgaṇaṁgeṟeyanāḷuttamiḻdalliya svayaṁbhukalidēvasvāmidēvargge
naṁdādīvigegaṁ śrīkhaṇḍadhūpakkamāyūraluḷḷa ghāṇadeṟeyuma sūḷedeṟe
manaṁda da sahitaṁ śrīmattraiḷōkyamalladēvaralli paramēśvaradattiyāgi paḍedu sa
khavarṣa 983 neya plavasaṁvatsarada jyēṣṭhadamāvāsayādityavāraṁ sūryyagrahaṇadaṁdu
koṭṭarī dharmmavanāvanōrvvaṁ pratipāḷisidaṁ vāraṇāsi prayāge kurukṣētrameṁbī
puṇyatīrtthaṁgaḷoḷ sāyira kavileyaṁ kōḍumaṁ koḷagumaṁ ponnalu kaṭṭisi dānaṁge
phaḷamakkumidanaḷidanā kavileyaṁ tapōdhanarumā tīrtthadallaḷida mahāpātaka
nakku . bahubhirvvasudhā dattā rājabhissagarādibhiḥ . yasya yasya yadā bhūmistasya
tasya tadā phalaṁ . Ahipatikaṁkaṇaṁge kalidēvasvayaṁbhuge koṭṭanāgra
hā ghāṇadeṟeyeṁbuvanaggaḷamanīna saraṇāgra
yāhaḷamana kalidaṇḍanāyaka
kēśavadaṇḍanāyaka śrīkavimukhadarppaṇaṁ
barmmadēvaṁ baredaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 9.125 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
